subject: Astronomy & Space
title: William Herschel: Discovering Uranus
description: A quick, practical guide to William Herschel: Discovering Uranus.

hook.text: In 1781, a musician-turned-astronomer found a new planet. It was the first planet discovered since antiquity.

buildup.text: William Herschel built his own telescopes. His 40-foot reflector was the largest in the world at the time.

discovery.text: He initially thought Uranus was a comet. Calculations proved it was a planet orbiting beyond Saturn.

twist.text: Herschel wanted to name it 'Georgium Sidus' after King George III. The international community chose 'Uranus' instead.

climax.text: He also discovered infrared radiation by splitting sunlight with a prism. A musician who changed two fields of science.

punchline.text: A musician found a planet. Then he discovered invisible light.

quiz.question: What did William Herschel discover besides Uranus?
options: Infrared radiation, by measuring heat beyond the red end of the spectrum
The rings of Saturn and their composition
The first asteroid in the asteroid belt
